questions.question: What does the AR in ARMA stand for?

explanations.a: AR stands for autoregressive, which models the current value of a time series as a function of its past values.
explanations.b: Auto-relative is not a valid term in this context.
explanations.c: Auto-random is not related to ARMA processes.
explanations.d: Auto-residual is not a valid term in this context.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the Yule-Walker method?

answers.a: To compute the Fourier Transform of a signal.
answers.b: To estimate the coefficients of an AR model.
answers.c: To calculate the inverse of a covariance matrix.
answers.d: To identify the frequency components of a signal.

explanations.a: The Fourier Transform is not related to the Yule-Walker method.
explanations.b: The Yule-Walker method is used to estimate AR model coefficients based on autocorrelation.
explanations.c: The method does not focus on inverting covariance matrices.
explanations.d: Frequency identification is not the primary purpose of the Yule-Walker method.

questions.question: What does the MA in ARMA stand for?

explanations.a: MA stands for moving average, which models the current value of a time series as a function of past errors.
explanations.b: Mean adjustment is not related to ARMA processes.
explanations.c: Matrix approximation is unrelated to ARMA.
explanations.d: Model analysis is not part of the ARMA acronym.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key assumption of ARMA modeling?

explanations.a: ARMA models require stationarity, not non-stationarity.
explanations.b: Wide-sense stationarity is a key assumption for ARMA modeling.
explanations.c: Periodicity is not a requirement for ARMA models.
explanations.d: An ARMA process does not necessarily have a zero mean.

questions.question: What role does the autocorrelation function play in the Yule-Walker equations?

answers.a: It estimates the noise variance in the model.
answers.b: It provides the coefficients for the MA part of the model.
answers.c: It is used to estimate the AR coefficients.
answers.d: It determines the order of the ARMA process.

explanations.a: The noise variance is not directly estimated from the autocorrelation function.
explanations.b: The MA coefficients are not determined by the Yule-Walker equations.
explanations.c: The autocorrelation function is central to estimating the AR coefficients.
explanations.d: The order is determined independently of the autocorrelation function.

questions.question: What is the general additive form of an AR(2) model?

explanations.a: Correct! This is the standard representation where the current value is a linear combination of the two previous values plus white noise.
explanations.b: Incorrect. While some DSP filters use negative signs for feedback, the standard statistical AR model is additive.
explanations.c: Incorrect. The signs are not mixed by default in the general definition.
explanations.d: Incorrect. This is a deterministic difference equation; an AR process must include the random noise term ε(t).

questions.question: What does the Yule–Walker method estimate in an AR process?

answers.a: The moving average coefficients of the signal
answers.b: The AR coefficients and driving noise variance
answers.d: The cumulative sum of past samples

explanations.a: Incorrect — Yule–Walker targets AR, not MA coefficients.
explanations.b: Correct — it estimates the AR coefficients φ_k and the driving noise variance σ_e^2.
explanations.c: Incorrect — zero-crossings are unrelated to Yule–Walker.
explanations.d: Incorrect — cumulative sums are not involved in AR parameter estimation.

questions.question: Why is the Yule–Walker matrix Toeplitz?

answers.b: Because each element depends only on the lag difference
answers.c: Because it is a diagonal matrix
answers.d: Because it contains only zeros and ones

explanations.a: Incorrect — rows are not identical, but symmetric in a special way.
explanations.b: Correct — Toeplitz structure arises since R[m-k] depends only on lag difference.
explanations.d: Incorrect — elements are autocorrelations, not just zeros and ones.

questions.question: How is the AR model order typically determined?

answers.a: By counting the number of poles in the PSD
answers.b: By the lag after which the autocorrelation decays to zero
answers.c: By selecting a p that balances model accuracy and noise variance
answers.d: By using the maximum amplitude of the signal

explanations.a: Incorrect — pole counting is related to frequency response but not order selection directly.
explanations.b: Incorrect — autocorrelation decay is not the sole criterion.
explanations.c: Correct — model order p is chosen to fit the signal well without overfitting noise.
explanations.d: Incorrect — amplitude is unrelated to AR order.

questions.question: Which feature of the AR process determines the shape of its PSD?

answers.a: The AR coefficients φ_k that define the all-pole filter
answers.b: The sample mean of the signal
answers.d: The maximum amplitude of the autocorrelation function

explanations.a: Correct — φ_k define the poles, which shape the frequency response and PSD.
explanations.b: Incorrect — mean does not affect the spectral shape.
explanations.c: Incorrect — residual sums are not used to compute PSD shape.
explanations.d: Incorrect — maximum autocorrelation is not sufficient to determine PSD.
